findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Hom.lean:58` defines `namespace BialgHomClass`, and lines `90-99` already put bialgebra morphism API such as `counitAlgHom_comp` and `map_comp_comulAlgHom` at that class-polymorphic level; `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Equiv.lean:60` shows `BialgEquivClass` extends to `BialgHomClass`.
findings.fix: Prove the pointwise lemma in `namespace BialgHomClass` with `{F} [FunLike F A B] [BialgHomClass F R A B] (φ : F)`, then derive the bundled `BialgHom` version as a specialization if desired.
findings.issue: `map_antipode` only accepts `φ : A →ₐc[R] B`, but the natural Mathlib level is any `F` with `[FunLike F A B] [BialgHomClass F R A B]`; this avoids requiring users of `BialgEquivClass` or other bialgebra-hom-like types to coerce manually.

summary: The Hopf-algebra statements are mostly at the right object level, but the morphism API is unnecessarily specialized to bundled `BialgHom` instead of Mathlib's existing hom-class convention.
